question_text: Two objects with different masses are dropped from the same height in a vacuum. Which of the following is true?

options.label: A
options.text: The heavier object falls faster because gravity pulls harder on more mass

options.label: B
options.text: The lighter object falls faster because it has less air resistance

options.label: C
options.text: Both objects fall at the same rate and reach the ground at the same time because acceleration due to gravity is independent of mass

options.label: D
options.text: The object with the larger weight falls faster because it experiences a greater gravitational force

explanation: Although heavier objects have larger weight (greater gravitational force), they also have greater inertia. These effects cancel out perfectly, giving all objects the same acceleration g in free fall. This is why a hammer and feather fall together in a vacuum.
